frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Yaar, I don't understand these guys who keep bothering girls even after they say no. My policy has always been simple: if a girl is not interested, move on. But agar woh taken hai, kisi ke saath committed hai, then it's a completely different level of respect. Uske boyfriend ya husband ko bhi toh respect karna padega na? That's basic decency. Her 'no' is one thing, but knowing she's someone else's? That's when you properly back off.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Deepak frames respecting a woman's existing relationship with another man (boyfriend/husband) as a 'different level of respect' and 'basic decency', implying her direct 'no' is less weighty than the claim of another male. This encodes the surface manifestation of stopping pursuit only after learning a woman is 'taken', not after she said no.
original_frame: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices and Home Cooking
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 39. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are posting on Facebook under your real name on Mother's Day. You are writing a long post about your mother. The post is emotional and specific. But every specific thing you mention is domestic Ñ her hands, her kitchen, her smell of her cooking, the way she stayed up waiting for you, the way she never complained, the way she put everyone first. You are writing this as a tribute. You do not notice that the woman you are describing has no identity outside her kitchen and her self-erasure and that you are presenting this as the ideal of womanhood to your 400 Facebook friends.
